---
title: "Patents, Papers, Parts & Planet"
subtitle: "Do student projects and academic research in synthetic biology form semantically related local innovation trajectories?"
---

## What this project asks

This project examines whether iGEM student projects and academic papers in synthetic biology
tend to occupy **related regions of semantic space** in the same cities.

The working idea is simple: cities that produce focused iGEM activity in a synthetic biology
subfield may also produce focused academic output in the same area — not because one causes
the other, but because both reflect the same local knowledge infrastructure.

We measure this using **SPECTER2 embeddings** (scientific text vectors) and **UMAP projection**,
then test city-level semantic overlap with a regression framework.

The primary case study is **carbon capture in synthetic biology** — a subfield large enough to
find examples across both artifact types.

::: {.callout-important}
## Language note
This project uses *descriptive* language throughout: *semantically related*, *co-located*,
*associated with*. We do not claim that iGEM projects cause related papers, or vice versa.
:::

---

## The corpus in numbers

| Artifact type | Count | Year range | Geocoded cities |
|---------------|------:|:----------:|----------------:|
| Academic papers | 8,720 | 2000–2026 | 1,146 |
| iGEM projects | 4,615 | 2009–2025 | 626 |
| **Total** | **13,335** | **2000–2026** | **379 shared** |
| Carbon-capture papers | 234 | 2005–2026 | — |
| Carbon-capture projects | 141 | 2011–2025 | — |

Papers were retrieved from OpenAlex using core synthetic biology keywords.
iGEM projects come from the iGEM Registry API.
All artifacts were geocoded to the city of their lead institution.

---

## Navigate the site

| Page | What you will find |
|------|-------------------|
| [Results](results.qmd) | UMAP semantic space · city-level overlap scores · regression table |
| [Carbon Capture](case-study.qmd) | Deep dive into the carbon capture subfield · 7 focal cities |
| [Methods & Data](methods.qmd) | Corpus construction · SPECTER2 embeddings · clustering · reproducibility |
| [Paper](paper.qmd) | Full manuscript |
| [Slides](slides.qmd) | Presentation slides |

---

## Source code

All code is available on [GitHub](https://github.com/Zer0Juice/synbiomap).
The pipeline is fully reproducible: see [Methods & Data](methods.qmd) for step-by-step commands.
